critical_issues.title: Critical Low Stock on High-Value Perishable Items
critical_issues.root_cause: Multiple high-demand items (Chicken Breast, Egg, Burger Bun, Capsicum, Green Chili, Dosa Batter) are critically below reorder levels despite being used in popular menu items. Stock levels are at 10-20% of maximum capacity.
critical_issues.recommendation: Immediate procurement needed for 6 items below reorder level. Implement automated reorder system with 2-day buffer for perishables. Set up daily stock monitoring dashboard with alerts at 25% above reorder level.

critical_issues.title: High Order Cancellation Rate
critical_issues.root_cause: 9 cancelled orders (3.5% cancellation rate) during Jan 27-28 period with total loss of ₹269,900. Pattern shows cancellations occurring during peak hours (12-13h and 19-21h) suggesting kitchen capacity issues or menu unavailability.
critical_issues.recommendation: Investigate cancellation reasons - implement pre-order confirmation of ingredient availability, add real-time kitchen capacity monitoring, and train staff on managing customer expectations during peak hours. Consider dynamic menu based on stock levels.

critical_issues.title: Excessive Kitchen Time Variability
critical_issues.root_cause: Kitchen preparation times range from 13.78 to 49.13 minutes (avg 32 mins) with high variance. Orders with similar items show inconsistent prep times, indicating workflow inefficiencies or staffing issues during peak periods.
critical_issues.recommendation: Standardize prep times by menu item, implement kitchen display system with time tracking, stagger prep for multi-item orders, add expeditor role during peak hours (12-14h, 19-22h). Target: reduce avg time to 22 minutes.

critical_issues.title: Overstocking of Non-Critical Items
critical_issues.root_cause: Multiple items stocked at 70-90% of max capacity (Paneer: ₹629,235, Cream: ₹648,100, Butter: ₹767,750) while critical items are understocked. Total excess inventory value of ₹2.8M tied up in slow-moving stock.
critical_issues.recommendation: Rebalance inventory policy: reduce Paneer/Cream/Butter orders by 40%, redirect budget to fast-moving items. Implement ABC analysis - prioritize A-items (high demand) with lower safety stock on C-items. Review supplier contracts for smaller, frequent deliveries.

critical_issues.title: Peak Hour Order Clustering
critical_issues.root_cause: 60% of orders concentrated in 2 time windows (12-14h and 19-22h) causing kitchen bottlenecks. Average order value during peak: ₹65,000 vs off-peak: ₹45,000, but longer wait times reducing customer satisfaction.
critical_issues.recommendation: Introduce happy hour pricing (14:30-18:30) with 15% discount to distribute demand. Prep high-volume items (French Fries, Garlic Bread) in advance during off-peak. Add 1 kitchen staff during peak hours. Implement order throttling at 12 concurrent orders.

critical_issues.title: Underutilized Average Order Value
critical_issues.root_cause: Average order value of ₹78,570 with significant variation. Many single-item orders (₹13,500-₹30,000) missing upsell opportunities. Takeaway orders average 25% lower than dine-in despite similar item mix.
critical_issues.recommendation: Train staff on combo suggestions (appetizer + main + beverage = 20% discount). Create value meals at ₹40,000-₹50,000 price points. For takeaway, implement minimum order value of ₹25,000 or add delivery fee. Digital menu with recommended pairings.

critical_issues.title: No Inventory Waste Tracking
critical_issues.root_cause: Data shows no recorded waste values despite having 15 perishable items with 2-7 day shelf life. Industry standard for restaurants is 4-10% waste. Missing waste tracking means unaccounted losses and inability to optimize purchasing.
critical_issues.recommendation: Implement daily waste logging system by item and reason (spoilage/prep error/overproduction). Install digital scales at waste bins. Set waste targets at <3% for proteins, <5% for produce. Weekly waste review meetings. Use data to adjust par levels.

critical_issues.title: Table Assignment Inefficiency
critical_issues.root_cause: Uneven table utilization - some tables (T01, T06, T07, T10) used 15+ times while others show minimal use. No clear table rotation strategy leading to server overload and uneven wear on furniture.
critical_issues.recommendation: Implement zone-based seating rotation system. Assign servers to zones, rotate zones weekly. Use reservation system to pre-assign tables based on party size. Track table turns per shift - target 4-5 turns during peak dinner service.
